purpose: DataFirst dataset index for ABM consumer agent calibration (BNPL / SA credit market)

datasets.title: National Income Dynamics Study 2017, Wave 5
datasets.notes: Longitudinal panel (Waves 1-5, 2008-2017). 39,434 individuals in Wave 5. Nationally representative. Sections include: income, expenditure, debt, personal ownership, loans, wages, grants. Best dataset for calibrating income distribution, DSTI ratios, and debt levels.

datasets.title: Income and Expenditure Survey 2022-2023
datasets.abm_relevance: PRIMARY - most recent SA income + expenditure + consumption data
datasets.notes: Most recent SA income/expenditure survey. Mixed diary + recall methodology. 31,042 dwelling units. National coverage. Critical for calibrating spending patterns and income distributions to 2022-2023 levels. API variable metadata not yet populated - data files available on portal. Predecessor IES 2010-2011 (zaf-statssa-ies-2010-2011-v1) has full variable metadata if needed.

datasets.title: General Household Survey 2022
datasets.abm_relevance: SECONDARY - income composition, social grants, household assets

key_variables.name: hwl_assets_*
key_variables.use: Household durable asset ownership (proxy for wealth tier)

datasets.notes: Annual cross-sectional survey. 2022 is most recent CAPI version (2020-21 were CATI due to COVID). Useful for income composition breakdown (wage vs. grant), asset-based wealth proxies, and household characteristics. GHS 2024 (zaf-statssa-ghs-2024-v1) also available.

datasets.title: Quarterly Labour Force Survey 2022, Quarter 2
datasets.abm_relevance: SECONDARY - employment status distribution, earnings by sector
datasets.notes: Quarterly survey tracking employment/unemployment. Useful for calibrating the share of employed vs. unemployed consumers, earnings distributions by sector, and probability of income shock. QLFS 2025 Q1 and Q2 also available (most recent). 161 variables.

datasets.title: All Media and Products Survey 2015
datasets.abm_relevance: SUPPLEMENTARY - financial product ownership (credit cards, store accounts, banking)
datasets.notes: 8079 variables covering product and service ownership/usage. Includes income brackets, demographic data, and product adoption. Variable names are cryptic without codebook - requires downloading with layout files. Useful for modelling which financial products consumers hold (credit cards, store accounts) and adoption patterns by income tier. AMPS was discontinued after 2015; replaced by PAMS. 2015 is the final year.

not_on_datafirst.data: Credit Bureau Monitor, Consumer Credit Market Report - quarterly aggregate credit data: total credit agreements, NPL rates, credit types, DSTI distributions
Quarterly Bulletin - household credit and debt statistics, household debt-to-income ratio, bank credit growth rates
FinScope South Africa Consumer Survey - financial inclusion, BNPL awareness, credit product usage by income band
